primary_diagnosis.name: Acne vulgaris with acute excoriation (excoriated acne) and behavioural distress

pathophysiology_summary: Underlying acne vulgaris (androgenic sebum excess, follicular hyperkeratinisation, C. acnes, inflammation) has flared and become pruritic. An itch-scratch cycle, amplified by the patient's intellectual disability and agitation, has produced excoriations with pinpoint bleeding and serosanguinous weeping, exposing raw dermis at high risk of secondary staphylococcal impetiginisation. Discriminators from mimics: lesions clearly originate from comedones/papulopustules (not primary impetigo), the patient is afebrile with no clustered punched-out monomorphic erosions (against eczema herpeticum), and there is objective primary inflammatory acne driving the itch (against delusional parasitosis). Management targets the itch-scratch cycle and barrier protection while screening for superinfection.

differential.evidence_basis: Itchy comedonal/papulopustular skin excoriated to bleeding.
Open bleeding lesions risk superinfection; screen for honey crusts, warmth, spread, fever.
Must be excluded in any acutely eroded facial eruption; here afebrile with no punched-out monomorphic erosions.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Attribute the picking to primary skin disease and screen for superinfection/eczema herpeticum; do not label primary psychiatric or delusional parasitosis.

differential.evidence_basis: Comedonal origin, afebrile, no honey crusts or punched-out erosions confirm excoriated acne.
Not established now but remains the key thing to safety-net given open wounds.
Working diagnosis: wound care, break the itch-scratch cycle, screen/ safety-net for superinfection.

red_flags.description: Signs of secondary infection: fever >38°C, spreading warmth/redness, honey-coloured crusting, purulence
red_flags.clinical_significance: Open excoriated skin can superinfect; these features would require antibiotics ± admission. Absent (afebrile, no crusts).
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Missed cellulitis/impetigo could progress to systemic infection.

red_flags.description: Uncontrollable agitation/self-harm risk not manageable by support staff
red_flags.clinical_significance: Ongoing self-excoriation risks severe skin trauma and scarring; escalation needed if unmanageable.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Labelling the picking as delusional parasitosis or purely behavioural and missing the treatable primary acne and itch.
Failing to screen open bleeding lesions for impetiginisation/eczema herpeticum.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Wound depth and whether crusting is honey-coloured on close inspection
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Urgent same-day in-person clinician review for wound care and assessment; ED/000 if superinfection or unmanageable agitation.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Treating as primary psychiatric disorder, or missing an early superinfection, are the main remote-assessment risks.

guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Manage acne with topical therapy ± oral agents; treat secondary infection if present; address excoriation and psychosocial contributors.
